tender_title: Consultancy Services for Data Analytics & AI Maturity Assessment

sections.heading: Executive Summary
sections.content:
ZIMA Solutions Limited is pleased to submit this proposal to Coop Bank Tanzania Limited for Consultancy Services for Data Analytics & AI Maturity Assessment.

ZIMA Solutions is a Tanzanian technology and business innovation company founded in 2020, specializing in digital transformation for financial institutions, government agencies, and enterprises. We have completed 20+ enterprise projects, served 50+ client organizations, and implemented 4 Bank of Tanzania integrations with 99.9% system uptime.

Our unique positioning for this engagement is our deep expertise in both AI/machine learning solutions and banking technology. ZIMA operates a production AI Agents platform (Zona) with 40+ industry-specific conversational AI agents, including financial services agents for microfinance, insurance, and banking. We also deliver core banking integrations (RTGS, TIPS, GePG, NIDA), loan management systems, and SACCOS/microfinance platforms — giving us first-hand understanding of data architectures, data quality challenges, and analytics opportunities within Tanzanian financial institutions.

This dual expertise — AI practitioner and banking technology integrator — positions ZIMA uniquely to assess Coop Bank's current data analytics maturity, identify AI readiness gaps, and deliver a pragmatic, actionable roadmap for AI-driven transformation.

sections.heading: Company Profile
sections.content:
ZIMA Solutions Limited is a Tanzanian-registered technology company incorporated on 17 January 2025 (Registration No. 181314605) under the Companies Act, 2002. The company operates from Makongo, near Ardhi University, Kinondoni, Dar es Salaam.

ZIMA is VAT-registered (TIN: 181-314-605) and holds a current ICT Services business licence (BL01396912024-2500021919) from Kinondoni Municipal Council.

Directors:
• Andrew Stanslaus Mashamba — Founder & CEO (70% shareholding)
• Caroline Ceasar Shija — Co-Director (30% shareholding)

Core Capabilities:
• AI & Machine Learning: Production AI Agents platform (Zona) with 40+ industry-specific conversational AI agents deployed across WhatsApp, web chat, and API channels
• Banking Technology: RTGS, TIPS, GePG, and NIDA integrations for financial institutions
• Enterprise Software: Loan management, SACCOS management, microfinance systems, API gateway
• Digital Payments: TanQR merchant systems, mobile money integration, payment reconciliation
• Data & Analytics: Data pipeline design, system integration, financial data processing

Performance Track Record:
• 20+ enterprise projects completed
• 50+ client organizations served
• 4 Bank of Tanzania integrations implemented
• 99.9% system uptime achieved
• 4.9/5 aggregate client rating (50 reviews)

sections.heading: Understanding of Coop Bank's Requirements
sections.content:
We understand that Coop Bank Tanzania seeks to engage a consultant to conduct a comprehensive Data Analytics & AI Maturity Assessment. This engagement is foundational to Coop Bank's digital transformation journey and will establish the baseline for data-driven decision-making and AI adoption.

Based on our understanding of Coop Bank's operational profile — a cooperative-focused bank with diverse product lines (personal, business, agricultural, cooperative accounts), digital channels (Coop Pesa mobile banking, internet banking, ATM network, M-Pesa integration), and multi-branch operations — we anticipate the assessment will need to address:

Data Landscape Assessment:
• Current data architecture across core banking, digital channels, and branch operations
• Data quality, completeness, and consistency across systems
• Data governance frameworks, policies, and ownership structures
• Data integration patterns between core banking, Coop Pesa, payment systems, and reporting
• Regulatory data requirements (Bank of Tanzania reporting, AML/KYC data)

Analytics Capability Assessment:
• Current reporting and business intelligence capabilities
• Descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics maturity
• Self-service analytics availability for business users
• Real-time vs. batch analytics processing capabilities
• Customer analytics and segmentation capabilities

AI Readiness Assessment:
• Technology infrastructure readiness for AI/ML workloads
• Data science and AI talent availability and skills gap analysis
• AI use case identification across lending, risk, customer experience, and operations
• Ethical AI and responsible AI framework assessment
• Vendor and partnership ecosystem for AI capabilities

Organizational & Cultural Assessment:
• Data literacy across the organization
• Data-driven decision-making culture maturity
• Change management readiness for AI adoption
• Executive sponsorship and strategic alignment

sections.heading: Proposed Methodology
sections.content:
ZIMA proposes a structured 4-phase methodology based on internationally recognized data analytics and AI maturity frameworks, adapted for the Tanzanian banking context.

Phase 1 — Discovery & Current State Assessment (Weeks 1-3):
• Kick-off workshop with Coop Bank executive team and IT leadership
• Stakeholder interviews across business units: Retail Banking, Corporate/SME Banking, Agricultural Banking, Cooperatives, Risk, Finance, Operations, IT
• Technology infrastructure audit: core banking system, data warehouse/lake, integration platforms, reporting tools
• Data inventory and cataloguing: identify all data sources, formats, volumes, and flows
• Review existing reports, dashboards, and analytics outputs
• Assess current data governance policies, data quality rules, and metadata management
Deliverables: Current State Assessment Report, Data Inventory Matrix, Stakeholder Interview Summary

Phase 2 — Maturity Assessment & Benchmarking (Weeks 3-5):
• Apply ZIMA's Data Analytics Maturity Model (DAMM) across 6 dimensions:
  — Data Strategy & Governance
  — Data Architecture & Infrastructure
  — Data Quality & Management
  — Analytics & Business Intelligence
  — Advanced Analytics & AI
  — Organization, Skills & Culture
• Score each dimension on a 5-level maturity scale: Initial → Developing → Defined → Managed → Optimizing
• Benchmark against Tanzanian banking sector peers and regional best practices
• Identify maturity gaps and prioritize improvement areas
• AI readiness assessment using ZIMA's AI Readiness Framework
Deliverables: Maturity Assessment Scorecard, Benchmarking Report, AI Readiness Assessment

Phase 3 — AI Use Case Identification & Prioritization (Weeks 5-7):
• Workshop-based AI use case ideation with business and IT teams
• Identify high-value AI/ML opportunities across:
  — Credit Scoring & Risk Assessment: ML-based credit models for cooperative and agricultural lending
  — Customer Analytics: Segmentation, churn prediction, next-best-product recommendation
  — Fraud Detection: Real-time transaction monitoring and anomaly detection
  — Process Automation: Document processing, KYC automation, loan origination
  — Conversational AI: Customer service automation across Coop Pesa and digital channels
  — Agricultural Analytics: Crop yield prediction, weather-linked lending, supply chain optimization
• Prioritize use cases using Impact-Feasibility matrix (business value vs. implementation complexity)
• Estimate data requirements, technology prerequisites, and investment for each use case
• Quick wins identification (< 3 months) vs. strategic initiatives (6-18 months)
Deliverables: AI Use Case Catalogue, Prioritization Matrix, Quick Wins Roadmap

Phase 4 — Roadmap & Recommendations (Weeks 7-8):
• Develop a 3-year Data Analytics & AI Strategic Roadmap for Coop Bank
• Technology architecture recommendations: data platform, analytics tools, AI/ML infrastructure
• Organizational recommendations: data governance structure, talent acquisition, training programs
• Investment estimate and ROI projections for prioritized initiatives
• Risk assessment and mitigation strategies for AI adoption
• Governance framework for responsible AI implementation
• Executive presentation and knowledge transfer workshop
Deliverables: Data Analytics & AI Strategic Roadmap, Technology Architecture Blueprint, Business Case Document, Executive Presentation

sections.heading: ZIMA's AI Expertise & Credentials
sections.content:
ZIMA brings proven, production-grade AI expertise to this engagement — not theoretical knowledge, but hands-on experience building and deploying AI solutions for East African businesses:

Production AI Platform — Zona AI Agents:
• 40+ industry-specific conversational AI agents in production
• Financial services AI agents: LoanGuide (microfinance loan status, repayment intelligence), InsureBot (policy management, claims), AccountantAI (invoice processing, tax intelligence)
• Healthcare AI agents: Dr. Amani (symptom triage, appointment management), PharmBot (drug interactions, prescription intelligence)
• Natural Language Processing (NLP) capabilities in English and Swahili
• Multi-channel deployment: WhatsApp, web chat widgets, RESTful APIs
• Handles real-time conversational flows with booking, payment, and data processing

Banking Data & Integration Expertise:
• RTGS Integration — Real-time gross settlement data processing and reconciliation
• TIPS Integration — Tanzania Instant Payment System data flows and analytics
• GePG Gateway — Government payment data processing and reporting
• NIDA Integration — National identity data verification and matching
• Loan Management Systems — Credit data models, repayment analytics, portfolio risk monitoring
• SACCOS & Microfinance Systems — Cooperative financial data architectures, member analytics

Data Engineering & Analytics:
• API Gateway platform — unified data integration layer connecting banks, mobile operators, and third-party services
• Payment reconciliation systems — cross-system data matching and exception analytics
• Financial reporting pipelines — regulatory (BOT) and management reporting
• 99.9% system uptime — production-grade data infrastructure reliability

This combination of AI product development experience and deep banking data integration expertise means ZIMA understands both the 'art of the possible' with AI and the practical data realities within Tanzanian banking institutions.

sections.heading: Proposed Team
sections.content:
ZIMA will deploy a focused consulting team with complementary expertise in AI, data analytics, and banking technology:

• Andrew Mashamba — Engagement Lead / AI & Data Strategy
  Founder & CEO of ZIMA Solutions. Architect of the Zona AI Agents platform. Deep expertise in AI/ML system design, banking technology integration (RTGS, TIPS, GePG, NIDA), and data architecture. Leads ZIMA's 20+ enterprise project delivery track record.

• Caroline Shija — Co-Director / Business Analysis & Stakeholder Engagement
  Co-Director of ZIMA Solutions. Experience in business process analysis, requirements gathering, and stakeholder management for technology transformation projects.

• ZIMA Data Engineering Team — Technical Assessment Support
  ZIMA's in-house data engineers and AI developers who build and maintain production AI agents and banking integrations. They will support the technical infrastructure audit, data inventory, and architecture assessment.

Additional specialist consultants (data governance, banking domain) will be engaged as needed to ensure comprehensive coverage of all assessment dimensions.

sections.heading: Deliverables Summary
sections.content:
The following deliverables will be produced during the 8-week engagement:

1. Current State Assessment Report — Comprehensive analysis of Coop Bank's existing data landscape, analytics capabilities, technology infrastructure, and organizational readiness

2. Data Inventory Matrix — Complete catalogue of data sources, formats, volumes, quality metrics, and data flows across all banking systems

3. Data Analytics Maturity Scorecard — Assessment across 6 dimensions scored on 5-level maturity scale, with gap analysis and benchmarking

4. AI Readiness Assessment — Technology, talent, data, and organizational readiness for AI adoption

5. AI Use Case Catalogue — Prioritized list of AI/ML opportunities with impact-feasibility analysis, data requirements, and investment estimates

6. Quick Wins Roadmap — Actionable initiatives achievable within 3 months for immediate value realization

7. 3-Year Data Analytics & AI Strategic Roadmap — Phased transformation plan with technology architecture, organizational design, investment, and ROI projections

8. Technology Architecture Blueprint — Recommended data platform, analytics tools, and AI/ML infrastructure aligned with Coop Bank's scale and requirements

9. Business Case Document — Financial justification for prioritized initiatives with cost-benefit analysis

10. Executive Presentation — Board-ready summary of findings, recommendations, and roadmap for Coop Bank leadership

sections.heading: Timeline & Investment
sections.content:
Engagement Duration: 8 weeks

Week 1-3: Discovery & Current State Assessment
— Kick-off, stakeholder interviews, infrastructure audit, data inventory

Week 3-5: Maturity Assessment & Benchmarking
— DAMM scoring, peer benchmarking, AI readiness assessment

Week 5-7: AI Use Case Identification & Prioritization
— Use case workshops, impact-feasibility analysis, quick wins

Week 7-8: Roadmap & Recommendations
— Strategic roadmap, architecture blueprint, business case, executive presentation

Key Milestones:
• Week 1: Kick-off workshop and project charter sign-off
• Week 3: Current State Assessment Report delivery
• Week 5: Maturity Scorecard and AI Readiness delivery
• Week 7: AI Use Case Catalogue and Quick Wins delivery
• Week 8: Final roadmap presentation to Coop Bank executive team

Investment: Detailed pricing to be provided in the Commercial Proposal section, denominated in Tanzania Shillings (TZS).

sections.heading: Why ZIMA Solutions
sections.content:
ZIMA Solutions offers Coop Bank the following differentiated value for this engagement:

• AI Practitioners, Not Just Advisors: Unlike traditional consultancies that advise on AI theoretically, ZIMA builds and operates production AI systems. Our Zona platform runs 40+ AI agents handling real conversations, payments, and data processing daily. We assess AI maturity from the perspective of practitioners who have shipped AI to production.

• Deep Banking Technology Integration: With 4 Bank of Tanzania integrations (RTGS, TIPS, GePG, NIDA), loan management systems, and SACCOS platforms, we understand Coop Bank's data landscape from the inside — the core banking data models, payment flows, regulatory reporting requirements, and integration challenges.

• Tanzanian Context Expertise: Headquartered in Dar es Salaam, we understand the local talent market, regulatory environment, infrastructure constraints, and technology ecosystem. Our recommendations are pragmatic and executable within the Tanzanian context, not imported from markets with different realities.

• Cooperative Banking Understanding: Through our SACCOS management and microfinance platforms, we understand the unique data and analytics needs of cooperative-model banking — member management, dividend calculations, democratic governance reporting, and agricultural lending cycles.

• Cost-Effective Local Delivery: As a lean Dar es Salaam-based firm, we deliver international-quality consulting at significantly lower cost than international firms, with no travel overhead and sustained local availability for follow-on support.

• End-to-End Capability: Beyond assessment, ZIMA can implement the recommended solutions — from data platforms to AI agents to banking integrations — providing Coop Bank a seamless path from strategy to execution.
